Name: Arachnophobia
Irrational Fear of Spiders Hitch
Arachnophobia, Fear of Spiders, irrational fear of spiders,
data.Description: Characters with Arachnophocia suffer from an irrational fear, terror, horror or fascination when in the presence of spiders and spider-like creatures.

Name: Irrational
Irrational Awe Hitch Type Proficiency
Irrationality, Irrational Hitches, Irrational Fears, Irrational Thoughts, Irrational Love, Phobias, Manias, Philias, Awe Hitch Type,
Prof.Description: Irrational Hitches cause a Character to engage in irrational behaviour, or experience irrational emotions.

Name: Fear
Awe Emotional Gnarl Proficiency
Alarm, Fear, Dread, Blanch, Cower, Creeps, Shudder, Quail, Flinch, Afraid, Fearful, Scared, Frightened, Wiggins, Phobia
Prof.Description: The emotion of fear is a distressing negative emotion aroused by impending danger, evil, or a chance of pain, etc. The feeling or emotional condition of being afraid.

Name: Stress
Stress (Orthodox Gnarl) Gnarl Proficiency
Stress, Stressful, Stressing, Orthodox Gnarl Proficiency
Prof.Description: The Gnarl of Orthodox is Stress. Stress is nervous energy that can be positively used, but in excess creates nothing but harm, but Stress is a normal response to any kind of adversity. Hitches that are triggered simply add Stress to the Character or Descendant. The Stress Gnarl will create Hitch Marks Stress for the Character or Descendant each time the Hitch is Triggered

Name: Instinct
Instinct Nature Gnarl Proficiency
Instinctive, Instinctual, Instinct, Natural Instinct, inherent, impulsive, innate, intuitive, ingrained, habitual
Prof.Description: The Gnarl of Nature is Instinct. When a Hitch with an Instinct Gnarl is Triggered the Hitch will take a predefined Action with a Score equal to the Hitch Banes, or Pips equal to the Hitch Marks. Instinct responses can be to flee (Flight moving away), flinch (prepare defences), flirt (or similar charm offence defence mechanisms or sexual activity), hide/vanish, or fight (attack whatever Triggered the Hitch). This Instinct cannot be broken or ammended once set, exposure to the trigger will always result in the same response, no matter the circumstances until the Hitch is Resolved or this Gnarl is removed

Name: Spider
Venomous Web-spinning Arachnid
Cob, Spider, Tarantula, Wolf-spider, Web-spinner, Wall-climber
Prof.Description: Spiders (order Araneae) are air-breathing arthropods, having an exoskeleton, that have eight legs, moved by a combination of hydaulic pressure and muscle contraction. They typically have chelicerae with fangs generally able to inject venom, and spinnerets that extrude silk which may be used to trap and bind prey or construct trap lines, hinges, webs, etc

Name: Cobweb
Lingering Dense Spider Webs
Cobwebs, Filament, Gossamer, Web, Webbing, Sticky Fibres, Snare, Spider-web, Spider-silk
Prof.Description: A dense mass of lingering, old, often dust-coated, abandonned or ill-kempt web, spun by a spider, as either a walkway or to ensnare prey. Anything finely spun or flimsy.

Name: Eight-Legs
Eight-Legged Arachnids
Arachnids, Spiders, Scorpions, Mites, Camel-Spiders, Ticks, Whip Scorpions, Pseudo-Scorpions
Prof.Description: All Arachnids, whether spiders, scorpions, mites, ticks or whatever are all categorized by having eight legs.

Name: Webs
Woven Interconnected Network Structure
Webs, Networks, Enveloping, Nets, Networks
Prof.Description: Webs are complex networks of fibres, often knotted or woven from individual fibres
